pattern: Numeric strings without leading zeros compare the same way numerically as they do lexicographically once you first break ties by length, so ranking the whole list only needs a custom sort key, never an actual big-integer conversion.
hints: These are numbers written as strings, possibly far too large to fit in a normal integer type, so comparing them numerically means comparing their string representations carefully rather than parsing them.
A longer digit string (with no leading zeros) always represents a bigger number than a shorter one, and among strings of equal length, ordinary lexicographic order matches numeric order exactly.
Sort the strings using (length, then lexicographic order) as the comparison key, from largest to smallest, and return the k-th one in that order.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, mixed one and two digit numbers
same length numbers with a lexicographic trap on the leading digit
a shorter-looking number that is actually larger due to string length
very long digit strings near the length cap

stress.description: random digit strings of varied lengths up to the size cap, with a random k
stress.note: no string above length 1 starts with a zero, matching real numeric strings
